plan.title: Add npm-handoff messaging to the Go installer success path

narratives.Description: The frozen Go installer is the last one consumers will run, so they must learn that future upgrades now come from the npm package. This story prints a handoff line on the installer success path in cmd/deft-install/main.go directing users to run npx @deftai/directive init for subsequent upgrades.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- Add a handoff message to the success summary path in cmd/deft-install/main.go so a completed install or upgrade prints the npm upgrade guidance to stderr.
- Define the message text as a constant near the existing installer source constants so the script keeps one definition, and gate it behind the non-error success path only.
- Add a Go test in cmd/deft-install that asserts the success output contains the npx @deftai/directive init handoff string and that an error path omits it.
narratives.UserStory: As a consumer running the final Go installer, I want a clear handoff message, so that I know future Deft upgrades come from the npm package rather than re-running the installer.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1972 step 1b; #1912 freeze prerequisite; #1942 npm-native init/update

items.title: Given a successful install or upgrade, when the installer finishes, then it prints a handoff line instructing the user to run npx @deftai/directive init.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a successful install or upgrade, when the installer finishes, then it prints a handoff line instructing the user to run npx @deftai/directive init.

items.title: Given an install that errors out, when the installer exits non-zero, then it does not print the npm handoff message.

narrative.Acceptance: Given an install that errors out, when the installer exits non-zero, then it does not print the npm handoff message.

items.title: Given the success path runs under test, when the output is captured, then the test asserts the npm handoff string is present.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the success path runs under test, when the output is captured, then the test asserts the npm handoff string is present.

expected_outputs: installer success output includes the npm handoff line
Go test asserting the handoff string on success and its absence on error

references.title: Issue #1972: Wave 5 finalization: freeze the Go bridge, retire Go, purge Python (runbook + recovery)
